title: Optimizer does not combine LogicalGets in UNION when both Scans have identical criteria
description: ### What happens? We have a duckdb-extension for our custom storage layer, and our query patterns have multiple analytics being (separately) requested for the same "base criteria" (i.e. "table_name", "pushdown-filters", "pushdown-projections") We are able to UNION the analytics into a single query; but the optimized plan still shows multiple LogicalGet nodes and our storage layer still receives multiple, identical read calls. ### To Reproduce ```sql CREATE TABLE INTEGERS(i INT, j INT, k INT); INSERT INTO INTEGERS VALUES (1, 1, 1); INSERT INTO INTEGERS VALUES (2, 2, 2); INSERT INTO INTEGERS VALUES (3, 3, 3); INSERT INTO INTEGERS VALUES (4, 4, 4); INSERT INTO INTEGERS VALUES (5, 5, 5); CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW view1 AS SELECT i, k FROM integers WHERE k IN (1, 2); CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW view2 AS SELECT j, k FROM integers WHERE k IN (1, 2); EXPLAIN ANALYZE FROM view1 UNION ALL BY NAME FROM view2; ``` ### OS: OSX ### DuckDB Version: v1.0.0 ### DuckDB Client: CLI ### Hardware: _No response_ ### Full Name: Paresh Thatte ### Affiliation: Bloomberg, LP ### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build. I have tested with a stable release ### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue? Yes ### Did you include all code required to reproduce the issue? - [x] Yes, I have ### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Python version, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue? - [x] Yes, I have
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE FLOATS(f FLOAT, g FLOAT, h FLOAT);
SELECT f, h FROM FLOATS WHERE h IN (3.3, 4.4) UNION ALL SELECT g, h FROM FLOATS WHERE h IN (2.2, 5.5);
CREATE TABLE STRINGS(s VARCHAR(255), t VARCHAR(255), u VARCHAR(255));
key_bug_features: Optimizer fails to combine identical LogicalGets in UNION
